Name: BMW
1Q: For what was BMW specialized before it began to make cars?
tip1: BMW first started producing aircraft engines, then motorcycles, and after that cars.
2Q: What is the name of the subsidiary of BMW AG that manufactures high-performance cars?
tip2: BMW M GmbH formerly was known as BMW Motorsport GmbH.
3Q: What was the first generation of the BMW M3 model?
tip3: The BMW E30 is the second generation of BMW 3 Series.
4Q: Which car in 1999 set a record for the fastest car of that time using BMW's V12 engine?
tip4: McLaren F1 achieved a top speed of 386.4km/h / 240.09mph.
5Q: BMW Sauber F1 Team has competed in Formula 1 for four seasons, with the only one win coming from who?
tip5: In June 2008, a Polish racing driver Robert Józef Kubica won the Canadian Grand Prix Formula 1 race.
6Q: What is the volume of the naturally aspirated V10 engine built into the M5 E60?
tip6: This engine has won several International Engine of the Year awards.
7Q: Which car was in 2017 set a Guinness world record for the Greatest distance vehicle drift?
tip7: BMW test driver Johan Schwartz drift for 8 hours for this title.
8Q: Which is the Bavarian manufacturer's first SUV?
tip8: BMW branded the X5 as a Sport Activity Vehicle (SAV) to emphasize its on-road ability.
9Q: What is the first BMW front-wheel-drive model?
tip9: The BMW 2 Series Active Tourer is compact Multi-Purpose Vehicle (MPV).
10Q: What turbo engine drives the first plug-in hybrid sports model i8 next to an electric motor?
tip10: BMW i8 has won many independent Car of the Year awards.
